tf: Cloud Computing enables on-demand network access to a shared pool of configurable computing resources.
Cloud Computing requires users to purchase physical hardware assets before using services.
The NIST definition of Cloud Computing includes five essential characteristics.
Elasticity in cloud computing means resources can be automatically scaled up or down based on demand.
On-premise infrastructure typically has lower upfront costs compared to cloud solutions.
Serverless computing eliminates the need for any servers to run applications.
Measured service in cloud computing provides metering and billing transparency.
Resource pooling in cloud computing uses a single-tenant model for each customer.
Broad network access restricts cloud resources to local area networks only.
The pay-as-you-go model shifts capital expenditure to operational expenditure.
mcq: Which organization published the standard definition of Cloud Computing?
Which of the following is NOT a NIST essential characteristic of Cloud Computing?
What does the pay-as-you-go model in cloud computing refer to?
Which term describes automatically scaling resources based on demand?
What is a workload in cloud computing?
An application or service running on resources
b) An application or service running on resources
Servers are abstracted away from developers
b) Servers are abstracted away from developers
Which computing paradigm preceded cloud computing historically?
On-premise vs Cloud: Which statement is correct?
c) Cloud shifts CAPEX to OPEX
Broad network access means cloud resources are:
b) Accessible over standard network protocols
Rapid elasticity in cloud computing refers to:
Quick provisioning and release of resources
b) Quick provisioning and release of resources
matching: Match the NIST characteristic with its description.
User can provision resources without human interaction
Resources accessible over standard network protocols
Resources can scale quickly up or down
Match the computing paradigm with its era.
Match the financial model with its description.
Match the cloud benefit with its explanation.
Automatic resource adjustment based on load
System continues functioning despite component failures
Match the workload type with its example.
Match the cloud characteristic with its requirement.
Match the economic factor with its cloud impact.
Overall cost including all direct and indirect expenses
Measure of profitability relative to investment
Reducing upfront spending while increasing flexibility
Speed of adapting to changing business needs
Match the historical development with its contribution.
Enabled resource abstraction and VM management
Match the deployment concern with its meaning.
Data processing rate per unit time
Match the service category with its focus.

tf: Serverless computing still requires the user to manage virtual machines.
A workload is a specific application or service that runs on cloud resources.
Scalability refers only to increasing resources, never decreasing them.
Cloud providers follow a pay-as-you-go financial model.
The evolution of cloud computing began with the invention of the internet in the 1990s.
On-demand self-service allows users to provision resources without human interaction.
Resource pooling means each customer gets dedicated physical hardware.
Rapid elasticity makes cloud resources appear unlimited to the consumer.
Measured service is optional and not required for cloud computing.
Utility computing is a concept where computing resources are delivered like electricity.
mcq: Which of the following best describes cloud computing?
On-demand access to shared configurable resources over the network
b) On-demand access to shared configurable resources over the network
What is the primary difference between CAPEX and OPEX?
CAPEX is monthly, OPEX is yearly
CAPEX is upfront investment, OPEX is ongoing expense
CAPEX is for software, OPEX is for hardware
b) CAPEX is upfront investment, OPEX is ongoing expense
Which NIST characteristic ensures resources can be monitored and billed?
What does abstraction mean in cloud computing?
Physical hardware is hidden behind virtualized software layers
a) Physical hardware is hidden behind virtualized software layers
Which of the following is an example of a workload?
What does multi-tenant refer to in cloud computing?
Multiple customers share the same physical infrastructure securely
Multiple VMs run on separate hardware
b) Multiple customers share the same physical infrastructure securely
Which paradigm shift preceded the cloud computing era?
What is the main advantage of shifting from CAPEX to OPEX?
Reduced financial risk and improved cash flow
b) Reduced financial risk and improved cash flow
Which of the following is NOT a benefit of cloud computing?
b) Quick automated allocation of resources
matching: Match the cloud deployment model with its description.
Services offered over the public internet
Dedicated cloud infrastructure for a single organization
Combination of public and private cloud
Shared infrastructure for organizations with common concerns
Match the cloud computing pillar with its definition.
Hiding physical hardware behind software layers
Automating deployment and scaling of resources
Creating virtual instances from physical hardware
Match the terminology with its correct meaning.
Shared pool of configurable computing resources
Application or service running on resources
Automatic scaling of resources based on demand
Match the characteristic with its opposite.
Match the NIST essential characteristic with its technical enabler.
Match the computing resource with its cloud abstraction.
Match the business benefit with its cloud enabler.
Pay-as-you-go and elimination of idle resources
Match the cloud service category with the provider example.
Match the historical era with its computing characteristic.
Match the financial term with its cloud relevance.
Total cost including direct and indirect expenses
Measure of profitability from cloud investment
Moving from upfront to operational spending
Financial management practice for cloud spending

tf: IaaS provides users with virtual machines, storage, and networking resources.
In PaaS, the user manages the operating system and middleware.
SaaS delivers ready-to-use software applications over the internet.
The Shared Responsibility Model means the provider is responsible for everything.
A public cloud is deployed for exclusive use by a single organization.
A private cloud can be hosted on-premises or by a third-party provider.
A hybrid cloud combines public and private cloud infrastructures.
A VPC is a physically isolated section of a cloud provider's network.
In the IaaS model, the user has full control over the operating system.
A community cloud is shared by several organizations with common concerns.
mcq: Which cloud service model provides the highest level of user control?
In the Shared Responsibility Model, who is responsible for physical security?
Which deployment model offers the best balance of cost and control?
Which service model abstracts the most infrastructure from the user?
What is the primary benefit of PaaS for developers?
b) Reduced management of underlying infrastructure
Which statement about the frontend of cloud architecture is correct?
It includes the client interface like browsers and CLIs
b) It includes the client interface like browsers and CLIs
What is the role of a load balancer in cloud architecture?
b) Distribute incoming traffic across servers
Which of the following is an example of SaaS?
What does the backend of cloud architecture include?
Application layer, service layer, and physical infrastructure
b) Application layer, service layer, and physical infrastructure

tf: Hypervisors are a key technology enabling IaaS.
In SaaS, the user is responsible for software updates and patches.
A private cloud can only be hosted on the customer's premises.
VPC allows users to define their own virtual network topology in the cloud.
PaaS eliminates the need for developers to manage server hardware.
In the Shared Responsibility Model, the customer always handles physical access controls.
A hybrid cloud must include at least one public and one private cloud.
Community clouds are typically more expensive than public clouds.
EC2 is an example of a PaaS offering from AWS.
Security groups act as virtual firewalls for cloud instances.

Flexibility to choose where workloads run

Physical isolation from other cloud users

Provide services like message queuing and databases

They control inbound and outbound traffic at the instance level
They are only in private clouds

b) A range of IP addresses within the VPC

tf: Virtualization is the foundational technology that enables cloud computing.
A Type 1 hypervisor runs on top of a host operating system.
A hypervisor is also known as a Virtual Machine Monitor (VMM).
Type 2 hypervisors provide near-native performance compared to Type 1.
VMware ESXi is an example of a Type 1 hypervisor.
A guest system refers to the physical machine running the hypervisor.
Live migration allows a running VM to be moved between hosts without downtime.
Hardware emulation virtualizes the CPU at the instruction level which is very fast.
Containers share the host operating system kernel.
Virtualization security risks include VM escape attacks.
mcq: Which type of hypervisor installs directly on physical hardware?
What is the main advantage of a Type 1 hypervisor over Type 2?
Which of the following is a Type 2 hypervisor?
What is a host system in virtualization?
b) The physical machine providing resources
Moving a VM while powered off
Moving a running VM to another host with minimal disruption
b) Moving a running VM to another host with minimal disruption
Which virtualization risk involves a guest OS accessing the hypervisor?
What is the function of a hypervisor?
Manage and isolate virtual machines on physical hardware
b) Manage and isolate virtual machines on physical hardware
Which statement about containers vs VMs is correct?
c) Containers share the host kernel
Simulating hardware components entirely in software
b) Simulating hardware components entirely in software
Which characteristic is unique to Type 2 hypervisors?
b) Requires a host operating system

tf: Paravirtualization requires modifying the guest operating system.
Hardware-assisted virtualization uses CPU extensions like Intel VT-x.
A single physical machine can host only one virtual machine.
VM snapshots capture the entire state of a virtual machine at a point in time.
Type 1 hypervisors have a larger attack surface than Type 2.
Virtualization enables better resource utilization by consolidating workloads.
Cold migration requires the VM to be powered off before moving.
Network isolation is automatically guaranteed in all virtualized environments.
The host OS manages hardware directly in Type 2 hypervisor setups.
Overcommitment of resources can lead to performance degradation.

All VMs running on it crash as well
VMs failover to another host automatically

Stores the guest OS and application data
b) Stores the guest OS and application data

Running multiple VMs on fewer physical hosts
b) Running multiple VMs on fewer physical hosts

c) Elimination of all security risks

tf: Microservices are an architectural approach where applications are divided into small independent services.
In a monolithic application each feature is deployed independently.
Microservices communicate with each other through shared databases.
Message queues enable asynchronous communication between services.
The Publisher-Subscriber model decouples message senders from receivers.
A broker is not required in a pub/sub messaging system.
Event-Driven Architecture relies on producing and consuming events.
Node.js fits serverless well because of its synchronous I/O model.
Microservices must all use the same programming language.
gRPC is an example of synchronous communication between microservices.
mcq: What is the main characteristic of a microservice architecture?
Small independent services each with a specific function
b) Small independent services each with a specific function
In the Pub/Sub model what is the role of a broker?
Route messages from publishers to subscribers
b) Route messages from publishers to subscribers
What is the primary advantage of event-driven architecture?
b) Loosely coupled and scalable services
Which is a disadvantage of microservices?
Middleware that lets services send and receive messages asynchronously
b) Middleware that lets services send and receive messages asynchronously
Which statement about synchronous communication is true?
The caller waits for a response before continuing
b) The caller waits for a response before continuing
What does the Publisher-Subscriber model enable?
One-to-many message distribution without sender knowing receivers
b) One-to-many message distribution without sender knowing receivers
Why does Node.js fit well with serverless computing?
Its event-driven non-blocking I/O handles short-lived functions efficiently
b) Its event-driven non-blocking I/O handles short-lived functions efficiently
What is the key difference between monoliths and microservices?
Microservices can be deployed scaled and developed independently
b) Microservices can be deployed scaled and developed independently

Routes messages from publishers to subscribers

Transports events from producer to consumer

Absorbs traffic spikes between producer and consumer

Single entry point for all microservice requests
Match the communication tool with its use.
Match the microservice deployment concern with its tool.

tf: In event-driven architecture services communicate by producing and consuming events.
A message queue stores messages permanently until manually deleted.
Pub/Sub requires publishers to know exactly which subscribers exist.
The broker in Pub/Sub routes messages to the correct subscribers.
Microservices always have lower operational complexity than monoliths.
Asynchronous communication eliminates temporal coupling between services.
In a monolithic architecture all features are intertwined in a single deployable unit.
Node.js is poorly suited for serverless because it is single-threaded.
An event in EDA represents a significant change in state.
Containerization is the only way to deploy microservices.

b) A named category for published messages

Store and forward messages between producer and consumer
a) Store and forward messages between producer and consumer

All services must use the same database

Single entry point that routes requests to appropriate services
Replace the need for service discovery
b) Single entry point that routes requests to appropriate services

Tracking a requests path across multiple services
b) Tracking a requests path across multiple services

tf: Containers share the host operating system kernel for resource efficiency.
Docker uses a hypervisor to run containers.
Namespaces in Linux provide resource isolation for containers.
Cgroups control groups limit and monitor resource usage.
The Docker Daemon (dockerd) is the client interface for Docker commands.
The kube-scheduler assigns pods to worker nodes based on resource requirements.
etcd is Kubernetes backing store for all cluster data.
Docker images are built in layers each representing a change.
Kube-proxy runs on the control plane node only.
mcq: Which Linux feature provides process isolation for containers?
What is the role of the Docker Daemon?
b) Background service managing Docker objects

What does a container runtime do?
Runs containers using Linux kernel features
b) Runs containers using Linux kernel features
Which namespace type isolates network interfaces?
What is the purpose of a Dockerfile?
Define the steps to build a Docker image
b) Define the steps to build a Docker image
What is the function of kubelet?
Agent on each worker node ensuring containers are running
b) Agent on each worker node ensuring containers are running
What does UnionFS enable in Docker?
Union of multiple filesystems into one
a) Union of multiple filesystems into one
The smallest deployable unit with one or more containers
b) The smallest deployable unit with one or more containers

tf: Block storage exposes raw sectors of fixed size to the host operating system.
File access model allows clients to access files and directories over the network.
DAS (Direct Attached Storage) can be shared across multiple physical servers.
NAS provides file-level storage access over a standard TCP/IP network.
SAN provides block-level storage access over a dedicated high-speed network.
RAID 0 provides data redundancy through mirroring.
Storage virtualization aggregates physical storage into a single pool.
Datastores are logical containers that hold virtual machine files.
NAS uses low-level SCSI commands for communication.
Storage thin provisioning allocates physical storage only when data is written.
mcq: Which storage architecture connects directly to a single host server?

Which storage uses Fibre Channel as a dedicated network?
What is a key advantage of NAS over DAS?
b) Multi-host file sharing over network
a) Redundant Array of Independent Disks
Abstracting physical storage into a unified logical pool
b) Abstracting physical storage into a unified logical pool

A logical container for VM files and templates
b) A logical container for VM files and templates
Which enables high availability through shared storage?
What is the main disadvantage of DAS?
No multi-host sharing leading to poor utilization
b) No multi-host sharing leading to poor utilization

tf: Object storage uses a flat namespace with independent objects.
Block storage supports partial updates without rewriting the entire file.
Object storage is best suited for low-latency database workloads.
The CAP Theorem states a distributed system can guarantee all three properties simultaneously.
Strong consistency guarantees every read returns the most recent write.
Eventual consistency means updates propagate over time and all copies converge.
Consistent hashing minimizes remapping when nodes are added or removed.
Amazon S3 is an example of block storage.
A distributed hash table (DHT) maps keys to nodes across a distributed system.
In a CP system availability is sacrificed when a network partition occurs.
mcq: Which cloud storage type provides virtually unlimited scalability?

According to CAP theorem which property is sacrificed in AP systems?
The remapping problem when nodes join or leave
b) The remapping problem when nodes join or leave
In cloud object storage how is an object updated?
The entire object must be rewritten (immutable)
b) The entire object must be rewritten (immutable)
What does a Distributed Hash Table provide?
b) Key-based lookup across distributed nodes
Which consistency model is typical for Cassandra?
What is a key advantage of object storage metadata?
Highly customizable with unlimited key-value tags
b) Highly customizable with unlimited key-value tags
Which is most suitable for backup and archival?

Multiple virtual positions per physical node
Circular arrangement of nodes for routing

tf: Big Data refers to datasets too large or complex for traditional databases to process.
The 5Vs of Big Data are Volume Velocity Variety Veracity and Value.
HDFS splits files into fixed-size blocks and replicates them across DataNodes.
In HDFS the NameNode stores the actual data blocks.
MapReduce consists of three phases: Map Shuffle & Sort and Reduce.
Apache Spark processes data in memory making it faster than MapReduce for iterative workloads.
YARN is responsible for distributed file storage in Hadoop.
MapReduce writes intermediate data to disk after each phase.
Spark RDDs are mutable collections of data that can be modified in place.
Apache Kafka is commonly used for real-time data ingestion in Big Data pipelines.
mcq: Which is NOT one of the 5Vs of Big Data?
What is the default replication factor in HDFS?
Which Hadoop component manages resources and job scheduling?
What is the primary advantage of Spark over MapReduce?
In-memory processing for faster iterative workloads
b) In-memory processing for faster iterative workloads
What is the role of the NameNode in HDFS?
b) Manage filesystem namespace and metadata
In MapReduce what does the Shuffle & Sort phase do?
Groups intermediate key-value pairs by key and routes to reducers
b) Groups intermediate key-value pairs by key and routes to reducers
What is a DataNode in HDFS?
Worker node that stores and retrieves data blocks
b) Worker node that stores and retrieves data blocks
Resilient Distributed Dataset - fault-tolerant collection of elements processed in parallel
b) Resilient Distributed Dataset - fault-tolerant collection of elements processed in parallel
What does HDFS rack awareness do?
Distributes block replicas across different racks to prevent rack-level failures
a) Distributes block replicas across different racks to prevent rack-level failures
Which tool is used for SQL-like querying on Hadoop?
matching: Match the Big Data V with its description.
Speed of data generation and processing
Transforms input into intermediate key-value pairs
Groups and sorts key-value pairs by key
Divides input dataset into independent chunks
Match Big Data pipeline stage with tool.
Match data processing pattern with framework.
Defers computation until action is called
RDD fault tolerance through transformation history
Match Hadoop ecosystem tool with category.
Data transfer between Hadoop and RDBMS
Match Big Data challenge with solution.
Processing tasks near where data resides

tf: OpenStack is an open-source cloud computing platform for managing compute storage and networking.
YAFS is a discrete event simulator designed for cloud topologies.
Docker can be used to deploy private cloud applications like Nextcloud.
MinIO is an example of a block storage solution.
Hadoop MapReduce jobs can be compiled and executed on a Hadoop cluster.
OpenStack Horizon is a command-line interface for managing OpenStack resources.
Docker Compose allows defining and running multi-container applications.
Taikun is a cloud management tool that can work with OpenStack.
Nextcloud provides file hosting and collaboration features similar to Dropbox.
MinIO is compatible with Amazon S3 API for object storage.
mcq: Which tool is used for provisioning cloud resources in the lab?
Discrete event simulation of cloud topologies
b) Discrete event simulation of cloud topologies
Which technology deploys Nextcloud in the lab?
What is the first step in deploying Nextcloud with Docker?
Set up Docker and Docker Compose
b) Set up Docker and Docker Compose
What does Hadoop MapReduce require to run?
A functioning Hadoop cluster with YARN and HDFS
a) A functioning Hadoop cluster with YARN and HDFS
Which component provides the web dashboard in OpenStack?
What storage protocol does MinIO use?
What is the role of Taikun?
A cloud management platform that works with OpenStack
b) A cloud management platform that works with OpenStack
Which is NOT a component managed by OpenStack?
